mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below 0.1pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05 over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above the 200 index threshold, persistent federal funds rate hikes force aggressive portfolio de-risking, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as real yields compress equity multiples.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates below 2.0% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day SPX returns in >60% of observations, or the Rank IC drops below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP print exceeding 2.0% annualized growth confirms robust macroeconomic demand, prompting immediate algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0%, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and stable or falling unemployment indicates a healthy supply-demand balance without overheating, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings visibility improves.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the predictive power disappears when the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%.
